purpose: Define a local, agent-readable and human-readable proof vocabulary for expressing mandate, evidence, intent, approval, freshness, scope, GatePass status, and review outcomes before action.
strategicPrinciple: We do not chase millions of AI agents. We create the trust rule they must satisfy.
safeCoreStatements: GatePass Trust Language gives agents a shared proof vocabulary before action.
This agent has presented proof for this specific action, under this specific scope, at this specific time.
Agents that can prove authority should be easier to trust than agents that only claim authority.
GatePass gives agents a language of proof.
Do not trust the agent. Trust the GatePass.
safePhrases: I am requesting permission to act.
My requested action is within this scope.
My GatePass is fresh and signed.
This action should be allowed locally.
This action should be blocked until proof is supplied.
This agent has presented proof for this specific action, under this specific scope, at this specific time.
No signed GatePass means no settlement in settlement-sensitive workflows.
unsafePhrases: Trust me because I am an AI agent.
This makes all systems accept the agent.
This should be used for viral agent promotion.
safePhrase: This agent has presented proof for this specific action, under this specific scope, at this specific time.
boundary: GatePass Trust Language is a proof vocabulary, not a safety guarantee. It does not create live systems contact, direct bot messaging, live agent-to-agent communication, hidden viral distribution, guaranteed trust, production certification, payment, settlement, real tool execution, or action execution.
